text.div: intentionally left empty / not meaningful

Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

td.span: Concussion with no loss of consciousness

tr.td: Human isophane insulin 70 unit/mL and recombinant human regular insulin 30 unit/mL solution for injection
Inject 10 units subcutaneously before breakfast and 10 units before dinner (strength: 70 units/mL isophane insulin and 30 units/mL regular insulin)
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Metformin hydrochloride 500 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Clopidogrel (as clopidogrel bisulfate) 75 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
400 micrograms (1 spray) under the tongue as needed for chest pain, may repeat every 5 minutes as needed, up to 3 sprays in 15 minutes
Metoprolol tartrate (as metoprolol succinate) 100 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Live attenuated Human alphaherpesvirus 3 only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Depression screening using PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire 9) score
Assessment using AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption)
History of coronary artery bypass grafting (situation)
History of coronary artery bypass grafting (situation)
History of coronary artery bypass grafting (situation)
History of coronary artery bypass grafting (situation)
Transfusion of packed red blood cells

ul.li: Achieve LDL cholesterol below 1.8 mmol/L in patient with ischemic heart disease and diabetes.
Achieve fasting triglycerides below 1.7 mmol/L.
Maintain HDL cholesterol above 1.0 mmol/L.
Achieve total cholesterol below 4.0 mmol/L.
Patient will achieve and maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Patient will implement recommended lifestyle changes (diet, exercise, sodium reduction, weight management) to support blood pressure control.
Maintain HbA1c at or below individualized target to reduce risk of microvascular and macrovascular complications.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce risk of cardiovascular and renal complications.
Maintain LDL cholesterol below 70 mg/dL to reduce risk of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease.
Perform self-monitoring of blood glucose as recommended to optimize glycemic control and detect hypoglycemia or hyperglycemia.
Implement dietary modifications, increase physical activity, and achieve weight management goals to improve glycemic control and overall health.

ul.li: Achieve LDL cholesterol below 1.8 mmol/L in patient with ischemic heart disease and diabetes.
Achieve fasting triglycerides below 1.7 mmol/L.
Maintain HDL cholesterol above 1.0 mmol/L.
Achieve total cholesterol below 4.0 mmol/L.
Patient will achieve and maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Patient will implement recommended lifestyle changes (diet, exercise, sodium reduction, weight management) to support blood pressure control.
Maintain HbA1c at or below individualized target to reduce risk of microvascular and macrovascular complications.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce risk of cardiovascular and renal complications.
Maintain LDL cholesterol below 70 mg/dL to reduce risk of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease.
Perform self-monitoring of blood glucose as recommended to optimize glycemic control and detect hypoglycemia or hyperglycemia.
Implement dietary modifications, increase physical activity, and achieve weight management goals to improve glycemic control and overall health.
